prestate_archive.reason: RV-1 checkpoint preserves the full pre-update queue in Git history; compact state restates active execution fields without treating the DSM-1 receipt as validated.

progress.note: BEM949-DSM-1 confirmed DONE via a genuine 2026-07-02 GitHub Actions run (run_id 28614313748), verified through two independent sources: the repository's own dispatch_lifecycle.jsonl (STATE_COMMITTED/COMPLETED/success) and an operator-supplied actual job log matching the same trace_id/task_id. The dsm1_finalize.py ts() bug at the time prevented this success from being converted into a receipt back then, causing the earlier misfile and confusion. That is now resolved. P7 preconditions are all satisfied.

tasks.resume_condition: GitHub Actions run-level outcome/log access or operator-supplied run URL/screenshot.

history.root_cause: dsm1_finalize.py had an undefined-name (ts()) bug at the time of the 2026-07-02 successful run, preventing the success from being converted into a receipt; fixed under BEM950-T6.

tasks.description: Resolve RULE-004..010 in rule_enforcement_matrix.json with code_enforced, policy_only, or not_enforced plus evidence.
limitations: This classification does not constitute Broad Release PASS.
This classification does not validate the separate DSM-1 runtime receipt.
RULE-005..010 remain policy_only and NOT_ENFORCED at runtime.

tasks.next_step: GPT must prepare P7 audit package v3 reflecting the now-genuine DSM-1 status. Final PASS/FAIL verdict on P7 is issued only by EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_CLAUDE after direct repository verification, not by GPT self-report.

tasks.description: Two real bugs observed in the 2026-07-04 DSM-1 dispatch attempts: (1) 'git pull --rebase' fails with exit 128 when the working tree has unstaged changes from the finalizer run — needs --autostash or an explicit stash/reset before rebase. (2) Multiple concurrent autopilot attempts race on writing governance/roadmap/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json, causing rejected pushes ('fetch first'). Needs either a retry-with-rebase-on-push loop or serialization (e.g. a lock file / concurrency group) so parallel runs don't collide.
acceptance_criteria: Fix both syntax errors (missing quote at inputs=... AND semicolon at force_task_id check), not just the one py_compile currently reports.
Fix the race_id/trace_id typo in current_receipt_matches().
python3 -m py_compile governance/runners/roadmap_autopilot.py governance/runners/dispatch_executor.py governance/runners/dsm1_finalize.py all succeed.
Original BEM950-T7 acceptance criteria (git rebase/race fixes) still apply and are not yet demonstrated.
tasks.note: GPT's first attempt at this task introduced a NEW file, governance/runners/roadmap_autopilot.py, which fails python3 -m py_compile. Two distinct syntax errors found by external audit (code read), not just the one py_compile reported: (1) reported by py_compile: `inputs={"trace_id: trace_id, "task_id": TASK_ID},` is missing a closing quote after "trace_id -> must be `inputs={"trace_id": trace_id, "task_id": TASK_ID},`. (2) NOT yet reported by py_compile (tokenizer never got that far): `if force_task_id and force_task_id != TASK_ID;` uses a semicolon instead of a colon -> must be `if force_task_id and force_task_id != TASK_ID:`. Fixing only (1) will still fail to compile because of (2). Additionally, a non-syntax logic bug: in current_receipt_matches(), `receipt.get("race_id")` should be `receipt.get("trace_id")` -- currently dead code (function is not called from main()), but wrong if ever wired in.

open_blockers.summary: GPT's T7 submission (roadmap_autopilot.py) fails to compile: two syntax errors (only one caught by py_compile so far) plus a race_id/trace_id typo in dead code.

next_action: GPT: fix ALL THREE issues in roadmap_autopilot.py in one pass (both syntax errors, not just the one py_compile reported, plus the race_id typo), then confirm py_compile passes for all three runner files. P7 audit package v3 can proceed in parallel since DSM-1/RV-1/RV-2/P1-ALT are unaffected by this bug.
